name: Circular de Frigiliana y Cómpeta
name_original: Sierra de Cómpeta – circular desde Frigiliana
description: This difficult 26.6-mile mountain bike route from Frigiliana in the Sierra de Cómpeta features 4016 feet of climbing and diverse terrain.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Frigiliana offers access to diverse terrain within the Sierras de Tejeda, Almijara, and Alhama Natural Park. This rugged mountainous region features undulating landscapes of pine and holm oak forests, dramatic limestone peaks, and crystal-clear streams. The area is characterized by significant elevation changes, providing challenging routes for mountain bikers. Views of the Mediterranean Sea and the Andalusian countryside are common along many trails. Best mountain bike trails around Frigiliana The most popular mountain bike trail is Circular de Frigiliana y Cómpeta, a difficult 26.6 miles (42.8 km) trail that takes 4 hours 21 minutes to complete. This route features varied terrain and significant elevation gain. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Cuesta del Río – Mountain bike trail near Nerja loop from Urbanización Ladera del Águila, a difficult 34.2 miles (55.0 km) path. It offers extensive riding through the natural park with over 1000 meters of climbing. Local mountain bikers also love the Frigiliana – Frigiliana Old Town loop from Villa Belydana, Casa Rural Villa Belydana, a 20.4 miles (32.8 km) trail leading through mountainous landscapes, often completed in about 3 hours 13 minutes. Mountain biking around Frigiliana is defined by rugged mountainous terrain, pine and holm oak forests, and limestone peaks. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Frigiliana are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 200 reviews. More than 1200 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Frigiliana's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Frigiliana?
A: There are 17 mountain bike trails around Frigiliana listed on komoot. These routes offer diverse terrain within the Sierras de Tejeda, Almijara, and Alhama Natural Park, catering to various skill levels.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Frigiliana?
A: The optimal time for mountain biking in Frigiliana is typically from &lt;strong&gt;October to May&lt;/strong&gt;. During these months, the weather is generally more favorable for physically demanding outdoor activities, avoiding the intense heat of the summer.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails in Frigiliana for beginners?
A: While the region is known for its challenging terrain, there are 8 moderate routes available. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40640975" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Páez Blanca Pass – Puerto Blanquillo loop from Frigiliana&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 27.6 km ride with significant elevation gain, suitable for those with some experience looking for a less strenuous option than the difficult trails.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Frigiliana?
A: The trails in Frigiliana traverse a rugged mountainous region characterized by undulating landscapes of pine and holm oak forests, dramatic limestone peaks, and crystal-clear streams. The Sierra Almijara, part of the natural park, features distinctive white marble and limestone rock formations, creating spectacular cliffs, ravines, and steep gorges. You can expect significant elevation changes and varied surfaces.

Q: Are there mountain bike routes with good viewpoints or landmarks?
A: Yes, many routes offer breathtaking panoramic views of the Mediterranean Sea and the Andalusian countryside. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/685880" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Fuerte Peak&lt;/a&gt; is a notable landmark providing exceptional 360-degree vistas. Trails also often pass by historical elements like ancient troglodyte dwellings and Moorish irrigation channels, and the exceptionally beautiful hamlet of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/381716" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Acebuchal&lt;/a&gt;, known as the 'Lost Village'.

Q: Can I find circular mountain bike routes around Frigiliana?
A: Yes, several circular routes are available. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994143327/circular-de-frigiliana-y-competa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circular de Frigiliana y Cómpeta&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 42.8 km trail. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14596286" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Frigiliana – Frigiliana Old Town loop from Villa Belydana&lt;/a&gt;, a challenging 32.8 km ride.

Q: Are there any trails that pass by waterfalls or offer wild swimming opportunities?
A: While specific mountain bike trails may not directly lead to waterfalls, the region is home to beautiful rivers like the &lt;strong&gt;Río Chillar&lt;/strong&gt; and &lt;strong&gt;Río Higuerón&lt;/strong&gt;, where visitors can find opportunities for refreshing summer walks, cool mountain pools, and cascading waterfalls. Wild swimming can be enjoyed in the Higuerón river and other cool mountain pools, often accessible via hiking trails that intersect with biking areas.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Frigiliana?
A: The mountain bike routes in Frigiliana are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of &lt;strong&gt;4.7 stars&lt;/strong&gt; from over 200 reviews. More than 1200 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Frigiliana's varied terrain, often praising the challenging routes, stunning views of the Mediterranean, and the diverse landscapes of the natural park.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain biking options in Frigiliana?
A: The mountain biking trails around Frigiliana are generally characterized by significant elevation changes and rugged terrain, with most routes categorized as moderate to difficult. While there are no specifically designated 'easy' or 'family-friendly' mountain bike trails listed, families with older children who are experienced riders might find the moderate routes manageable. Always check the specific route details for elevation and technical difficulty before setting out.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter on the trails?
A: The Sierras de Tejeda, Almijara, and Alhama Natural Park boasts a rich biodiversity. While mountain biking, you might spot various wildlife such as mountain goats (Ibex), wild boars, foxes, eagles, vultures, and kestrels. The diverse ecosystems make it an excellent area for wildlife watching.

Q: Are there bike rental services available in Frigiliana?
A: Yes, bike rental services are available in the Frigiliana area, making it convenient for visitors who wish to explore the trails without bringing their own equipment.

Known as the lost town of Malaga, this town was forcibly uninhabited during the Franco regime because its neighbors had helped the anti-Franco guerrilla "Maquis". Many decades later it began to be rehabilitated and today it is beginning to re-emerge as a remote tourist destination, despite its proximity to the popular Frigiliana.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This challenging mountain bike route is approximately 42.8 kilometers long with significant elevation changes. Komoot estimates the duration to be around 4 hours and 28 minutes of riding time, but allow extra time for breaks, enjoying the views, and navigating technical sections.

faqs.question: Is the Sierra de Cómpeta circular mountain bike route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is classified as difficult and requires very good fitness and advanced riding skills. Riders should be prepared for rugged terrain, steep sections, and potential bike pushing. It is not recommended for beginners.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the Sierra de Cómpeta circular route?
faqs.answer: The route features a diverse mix of surfaces, including gravel, paved, unpaved, and asphalt sections. You'll encounter paths, roads, streets, access roads, and singletrack. Beyond the Daire track, the terrain becomes particularly rugged with steep footpaths and sandy, pine-covered slopes, characteristic of an 'Alpine kind of landscape'.

faqs.question: What are the main scenic highlights or viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll experience breathtaking panoramic views of the Sierra Tejada and Sierra de Almijara mountain ranges. Key viewpoints include those around Cruz de Canillas. The route also offers views down into gorges like the Moreno gorge and passes through areas with tall limestone dolomitic mountains and ancient stone wall terracing of deserted vineyards.

faqs.question: Are there any notable villages or landmarks along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes through or near several interesting points. You'll start from Frigiliana Old Town, a beautiful white village. A notable stop is the abandoned village of El Acebuchal village. You'll also cross the Torrox River Crossing and traverse the Páez Blanca Pass.

faqs.question: Where can I park when starting this mountain bike route from Frigiliana?
faqs.answer: Frigiliana has several public parking areas, particularly around the entrance to the old town. It's advisable to arrive early, especially during peak season, to secure a spot as parking can fill up quickly.

faqs.question: Is the Sierra de Cómpeta circular mountain bike route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally permitted on trails within the Natural Park, it's crucial to keep them on a leash, especially given the presence of wildlife and the challenging terrain. Ensure your dog is fit enough for a long, difficult ride and carry enough water for both of you.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to ride this trail is typically during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) months. Temperatures are milder, and the landscape is often at its most vibrant. Summers can be very hot, making the long climbs particularly strenuous, while winters can bring cooler temperatures and occasional rain.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits to mountain bike in the Sierra de Cómpeta Natural Park?
faqs.answer: Generally, no specific permits are required for mountain biking on established trails within the Sierra de Cómpeta Natural Park. However, always respect park regulations, stay on marked paths, and practice Leave No Trace principles to protect the natural environment.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this circular route forms a segment of the Great Malaga Path (GR 249), specifically part of Stage 6. It also incorporates sections of other local trails such as Frigiliana - Cómpeta, Cómpeta - Frigiliana, Ruta Venta Cebolleros, and Ruta Acebuchal.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or water sources along the route?
faqs.answer: The route passes through the abandoned village of El Acebuchal village, which has been partially restored and offers a restaurant. However, for the majority of the trail, especially in the more remote sections, there are no facilities or reliable water sources. It is essential to carry sufficient water and supplies for the entire duration of your ride.
